steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage asserts that 31 blackspotted rockskippers at station 3 was not the lowest count at any station. To complete that assertion, the blank must supply a count that is lower than 31 — proving that some other species/station combination had a smaller total.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — there were 16 streaky rockskippers observed at station 1.: 16 < 31, so it could work in principle — but the table shows no entry of 16 streaky rockskippers at station 1. This misquotes the data. ✗
  • Choice B — there were 610 streaky rockskippers observed at station 3.: 610 > 31, so it is higher than the count in question — this would actually show 31 is not uniquely low, not prove it is not the lowest. ✗
  • Choice C — there were 16 barred flagtails observed at station 3.: 16 < 31, and the table confirms this count. A value below 31 at station 3 proves that 31 was not the lowest count there. ✓
  • Choice D — there were 50 Cocos frillgobies observed at station 1.: 50 > 31, so it is higher than 31 and does nothing to support the claim. ✗
C is correct.
